chapter_context.summary: Amaziah of Judah reigns well, defeats Edom, but is defeated by Jehoash of Israel in a foolish war; Jeroboam II reigns wickedly in Israel but restores Israel's borders in a prosperous era.

outline.text: King Amaziah's Good Reign in Judah; Punishes Father's Murderers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Amaziah's Good Reign in Judah; Punishes Father's Murderers (14:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Civil War Between Amaziah and Jehoash; Judah Defeated; Jerusalem Walls Tattered
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Civil War Between Amaziah and Jehoash; Judah Defeated; Jerusalem Walls Tattered (14:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Death of Jehoash; Conspiracy Against Amaziah; Assassinated; Azariah Reigns
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Death of Jehoash; Conspiracy Against Amaziah; Assassinated; Azariah Reigns (14:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jeroboam II's Prosperous Reign in Israel; Recovers Territories
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam II's Prosperous Reign in Israel; Recovers Territories (14:23-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 14 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
